page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it includes a 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1892.08 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1601.0. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance is relatively weak, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3862.58 and a poor bzip3 multi-threaded decompression score of 19.62 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth tests show a weak read-only large block speed of 30.03 GB/sec, though write performance for large blocks remains average at 25.36 GB/sec. The server demonstrates average capabilities in static web serving throughput at 6.34 GB/sec and single-threaded gzip compression at 28.23 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6idn.xlarge provides balanced general-purpose compute resources with the added value of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage. This resource density makes it suitable for workloads that require a balance of compute and memory alongside fast local temporary storage, such as static web serving, light text processing, and basic single-threaded data compression tasks. It is less optimal for highly parallelized multi-threaded processing or memory-intensive database operations due to its lower multi-core and read bandwidth benchmark ratings.
description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It includes a 237 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average single-core CPU capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1601.0, but weaker multi-core scaling, as reflected in its stress-ng multi-core score of 3862.58. Memory read bandwidth is also limited, though large block write speeds and static web serving throughput of 6.34 GB/sec remain average. This server is best utilized for workloads requiring balanced compute and fast local storage, such as static web serving, single-threaded data processing, and light text processing, rather than highly parallelized or database-heavy applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.xlarge is a general-purpose server with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD, offering balanced single-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.xlarge features 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A general-purpose x86 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed local NVMe storage, and balanced single-core performance.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 3.5 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 237 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Average single-core performance but weaker multi-core scaling in benchmarks
Suitable for static web serving, text processing, and single-threaded tasks
